case_id: Electricity, bituminous coal, at power plant

pi_items.source: Task description (line 5) specifies 'Carbon Liability (USD)' but metadata omits '(USD)'
Conversion calculation: $100/metric tonne ÷ 1000 kg/tonne = $0.1/kg USD verified by multiple carbon tax references
Metadata uses GWP=25 (IPCC AR4, 2007) which is outdated. Current IPCC AR6 (2021) specifies GWP=27.0 (non-fossil) or 29.8 (fossil). Correct calculation: 0.1 USD/kg × 27.0 = 2.7 or 0.1 × 29.8 = 2.98
Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 13-14) which creates /home/ga/LCA_Results directory and export_result.sh (line 12) which defines RESULT_FILE path

USLCI is a real, publicly available database from NREL (National Renewable Energy Laboratory) containing gate-to-gate, cradle-to-gate, and cradle-to-grave LCI data for U.S. processes. Confirmed via official NREL USLCI website and Federal LCA Commons
Task specifies 'Carbon dioxide' (emission to air) and 'Methane' (emission to air) flows. USLCI database contains standard GHG emission flows for all industrial processes
Task description (line 5) explicitly states 'carbon tax of $100.00 USD per metric tonne of CO2-equivalent'

Task uses IPCC AR4 (2007) value of 25. Current standard is IPCC AR6 (2021) with 27.0 for non-fossil methane and 29.8 for fossil methane. IPCC AR5 used 28. The GWP value of 25 is 13-19% lower than current scientific consensus.

privileged_info_summary: The task specifies a custom LCIA method 'Corporate Shadow Price 2026' with carbon tax of $100/metric tonne USD. The CO2 characterization factor of 0.1 USD/kg is correctly calculated by converting $100/tonne to $/kg (verified via carbon pricing sources). However, the methane factor of 2.5 uses an outdated GWP value of 25 (IPCC AR4, 2007). The current IPCC AR6 (2021) standard specifies GWP of 27.0 for non-fossil methane or 29.8 for fossil methane, making the correct methane factor 2.7-2.98 USD/kg, not 2.5. The category name in metadata ('Carbon Liability') omits '(USD)' specified in the task description. The USLCI database is confirmed as a real, public database from NREL containing the required emission flows. The result file path and all setup scripts are consistent with the specified locations.
